_meta.editorial_review_notes:
Provenance notes for this specimen.
AI-drafted fields are marked with `ai_drafted: true` in each row's field_provenance block.

AI-drafted field counts in this specimen:
  - 27 × markets.underlying_reference
  - 1 × events.editorial_notes
  - 1 × events.tags

Provenance rules:
  - Kalshi `resolution_source_name` and `resolution_source_url` are always pulled from the series `settlement_sources` API field.
  - Polymarket `resolution_source_name` uses an editorial default ('Credible news reporting — subjective') only when the platform API ships the field empty.
  - `underlying_reference`: per-market AI draft, anchored on market close_at for authoritative dates.
  - `editorial_notes`: per-event AI draft, 2-3 sentences of institutional framing.
  - `tags`: 4-6 per event, mix of thematic, attribute, and entity tags, anchored on the authoritative year from close_at.
  - `question`: AI rewrite of raw platform prose into canonical grammar when needed; otherwise platform value retained.

events.question: Where will the S&P 500 close on December 31, 2026?
events.editorial_notes: These markets track the S&P 500 closing level on December 31, 2026, as published by S&P Dow Jones Indices. Kalshi structures exposure as a discrete strike ladder, pricing probability within each 200-point band. The cross-platform insight is structural: Kalshi prices index level via contiguous range brackets, while any directional overlay prices above/below a single threshold. Resolution relies on Kalshi staff determination against the official S&P Dow Jones Indices close.

markets.question_raw: Will the S&P 500 be above 9000 on Dec 31, 2026 at 4pm EST?
markets.underlying_reference: The S&P 500 closing price on December 31, 2026, as published by S&P Dow Jones Indices.
markets.resolution_rules_raw:
If the S&P 500 index value on Dec 31, 2026 at 4pm EST is above 9000, then the market resolves to Yes.

The market will close on December 31, 2026. The market will expire at the sooner of the first release of the data, or one week after December 31, 2026.

Pursuant to the Kalshi Rulebook, the Exchange has modified the Source Agency and Underlying for indices markets. See the rules for more information.
